```python
class Solution:
    def lastSubstring(self, s: str) -> str:
        n = len(s)
        max_str = ""
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i + 1, n + 1):
                substr = s[i:j]
                if substr > max_str:
                    max_str = substr
        return max_str
```
efficiency: LOW
time complexity: O(n^3)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: HIGH
explanation: This solution generates all substrings of the input string and compares them to find the lexicographically maximum substring. It has a high time complexity due to the nested loops and string comparison.
